events.title: An Evening of Bliss: Yin Yoga, Craniosacral, Massage & Tea
events.description: A Candlelit Journey of Deep Rest, Healing Touch & Nervous System ResetStep into the soft glow of candlelight and let the world melt away...Join Aleena McKinnon of Buddha Beats Yoga and Eline Vary, Registered Massage Therapist & Craniosacral Practitioner, for a deeply nourishing 75-minute experience that blends Yin Yoga, massage, and craniosacral therapy — all held in a serene, candlelit space designed to soothe your senses and restore your soul.What to Expect:In this slow and meditative

raw.title: An Evening of Bliss: Yin Yoga, Craniosacral, Massage & Tea
raw.description:
A Candlelit Journey of Deep Rest, Healing Touch & Nervous System Reset

Step into the soft glow of candlelight and let the world melt away...

Join Aleena McKinnon of Buddha Beats Yoga and Eline Vary, Registered Massage Therapist & Craniosacral Practitioner, for a deeply nourishing 75-minute experience that blends Yin Yoga, massage, and craniosacral therapy — all held in a serene, candlelit space designed to soothe your senses and restore your soul.

What to Expect:

In this slow and meditative class, you’ll be guided through a series of gentle Yin Yoga postures, held for several minutes to release tension and open the body from deep within. As you rest in each shape, Elaine will offer hands-on massage and craniosacral therapy, intuitively supporting your body with skilled, healing touch.

Set entirely by candlelight, this immersive experience invites you to fully surrender — physically, emotionally, and energetically — into a space of peace and inner stillness.

After-Class Tea & Connection

To close the evening, we’ll gather for a warm cup of herbal tea and a brief connection, giving space to integrate the experience, connect with the community, or sit in silence and enjoy the stillness. It’s a gentle way to land back into your body — and into the present moment — before you float off into the rest of your evening.

With years of experience in massage therapy and a gift for craniosacral healing, Eline offers a nurturing, intuitive presence that makes you feel completely supported and cared for.

raw.title: Artist Development Series: Art and Accountability on sʔuknaqinx / syilx / Okanagan Territory
raw.admission: Pay It Forward/If You Can (Please register on website link.)
raw.description:
The Art & Accountability on sʔuknaqinx / syilx / Okanagan Territory session is a foundational learning experience designed for settler and non-Indigenous artists, hosted in direct partnership with the Sncewips Heritage Museum. This session moves toward meaningful action by grounding creative practices in respect, truth, and the historical context of the unceded, ancestral territory of the sʔuknaqinx/syilx people. Led by expert museum staff, participants will take part in a curated tour focused on the living history, sovereignty, and enduring presence of the sʔuknaqinx/syilx peoples.

Following the tour, the session facilitates an in-depth discussion on ethical creative practices, helping artists distinguish between cultural appreciation and appropriation to avoid the extraction of Indigenous narratives. Central to this work is an exploration of positionality, providing creators with the tools to identify their roles as guests on the land and reflect this authentically in their artist statements and professional practices. By moving beyond symbolic gestures, the program empowers creators to transform their work into an act of reciprocity and have genuine engagement within a sʔuknaqinx/syilx/Okanagan space such as the museum.

This workshop is free to attend. However, for those with the capacity to do so, we invite you to participate in our Pay It Forward program.

events.title: BEETHOVEN’S FIFTH
events.description: Experience the symphony that changed music forever. Shocking at the time of its premiere with its emotional intensity, today Beethoven’s 5th is one of the world’s most recognizable works of art. After more than a decade, the OSO returns to this iconic masterpiece to close the season with a monument of musical expression. This concert also features the Okanagan Symphony Chorus in Ola Gjeilo’s Sunrise Mass, a profound and expressive journey, full of soaring melodies and walls of sonic power.

events.title: Blossom & Bites: A Spring Lunch for Two
events.description:
A Springtime Meadery lunch for TWO! Enjoy the Spring Sunshine on our farm! Start with a Local, Charcuterie Bento Lunch in our Picnic Area or our picturesque outdoor Bistro and a guided wine tasting or flight of our honey wines. raw.title: Blossom & Bites: A Spring Lunch for Two
raw.description:
A Springtime Meadery lunch for TWO! Enjoy the Spring Sunshine on our farm! Start with a Local, Charcuterie Bento Lunch in our Picnic Area or our picturesque outdoor Bistro and a guided wine tasting or flight of our honey wines. Take our self-guided Bee Tour, take the Honeybee Challenge, play lawn games & enjoy farm fun.  Finish your experience with a scrumptious local Bernard Callebaut Chocolate Brownie with homemade honey ice cream for two! 

Includes:

Bento Local Charcuterie for two

Local meats & cheeses, house-made pickles, fresh fruit, house-made organic crostinis, homemade honey jams & accoutrements

2 Guided Wine Tastings featuring five of our award-winning honey wines  (local mocktails for non-drinkers or under 19!)

Bernard Callebaut Chocolate Brownie dessert with homemade honey ice cream to share

Self-Guided Bee Tour

Lawn games and picnic area

Take the Honeybee Challenge

Entry into our Spring Draw

*Please select a time slot between 11:30 am and 3:30 pm. Tickets required. We look forward to seeing you at the winery!

events.title: Cooking Class: Mother's Day Series
events.description: Celebrate Mother’s Day in the most meaningful way—gathered in the kitchen, cooking side by side.In this special hands-on class, you’ll be guided through cherished Italian recipes inspired by Nonna’s table—simple, soulful, and rooted in tradition.Learn time-honoured techniques as you create comforting, from-scratch dishes that bring people together. From rolling fresh pasta to finishing with a classic dessert, this experience is all about connection, tradition, and sharing a beautiful meal.Cook,

raw.title: Cooking Class: Mother's Day Series
raw.description:
Celebrate Mother’s Day in the most meaningful way—gathered in the kitchen, cooking side by side.

In this special hands-on class, you’ll be guided through cherished Italian recipes inspired by Nonna’s table—simple, soulful, and rooted in tradition.

Learn time-honoured techniques as you create comforting, from-scratch dishes that bring people together. From rolling fresh pasta to finishing with a classic dessert, this experience is all about connection, tradition, and sharing a beautiful meal.

Cook, laugh, and sit down together to enjoy a heartfelt Mother’s Day celebration—just like Nonna would.

raw.title: Drop In DIY Sign Painting
raw.description:
Join us for an afternoon down town Kelowna creating personalized wood signs and home decor.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Reserve Your seat online or by calling Lasma
- Drop in will also be open if there is space available the day of Crafternoon:)

When you arrive at the studio you will:
- Choose your project
- Design from the design catalog
- colours

You will be able to bring the project home with you the same day.

Each person attending must be creating a project except kids 6 and under can create one project together with a parent.

events.title: Elli Hart and Tess Anderson
events.description: Alt-rock energy meets haunting indie power on the Red Bird stageLive music in Kelowna continues Saturday, May 9, as Elli Hart and Tess Anderson share the stage for a powerful night of indie rock and alt rock at Red Bird Brewing. Music runs from 8 pm to 11 pm for a special ticketed show featuring two Canadian artists known for emotionally charged songwriting and unforgettable live performances.

events.title: Floor Opener
events.description: Lawrence Paul Yuxweluptun is one of Canada’s most outspoken and influential contemporary artists, confronting colonial suppression, environmental degradation, and the ongoing struggles for Indigenous sovereignty. Of Cowichan (Hul’q’umi’num Coast Salish) and Okanagan (Syilx) descent, his forty-year practice brings together traditional iconography, fearless critique, and biting satire to challenge and unsettle. Floor Opener presents a selection of works that are at once polemical and playful, imbu

raw.title: Floor Opener
raw.description: Lawrence Paul Yuxweluptun is one of Canada’s most outspoken and influential contemporary artists, confronting colonial suppression, environmental degradation, and the ongoing struggles for Indigenous sovereignty. Of Cowichan (Hul’q’umi’num Coast Salish) and Okanagan (Syilx) descent, his forty-year practice brings together traditional iconography, fearless critique, and biting satire to challenge and unsettle. Floor Opener presents a selection of works that are at once polemical and playful, imbued with a spiritual resonance that speaks urgently to the political and social realities of our time. This nationally significant exhibition is organized and circulated by the Kitchener-Waterloo Art Gallery, with artwork sourced from private collections in British Columbia.

events.title: Garden Party Festival
events.description: Garden Party unfolds as a 360-degree open-air house music experience at the center of Kelowna’s City Park. Curated sound, exceptional pours, and a sunlit dancefloor set the stage for a day defined by music, style, and connection.

raw.title: Geology & Landforms Hike
raw.description:
Free Program - Registration Required to secure your spot: https://www.rdco.intellionline.ca/WEBSITE/public/category/browse/GEOLOGY

Take a guided hike into the Regional Parks most known for their unique geological features. We’ll uncover the geological forces that shaped the Okanagan landscapes and learn about the rock cycle, geology basics, and the natural history beneath your feet.

For more information on other programs offered by the RDCO and registration details, please visit our website: https://www.rdco.intellionline.ca/WEBSITE/public.
